tool_args.label: Staff Summary - Grant of Easement - Breakneck Connector

payload.title: GRANT OF EASEMENT TO NEW YORK STATE PARKS, RECREATION & HISTORIC PRESERVATION, AND NEW YORK CITY DEPARTMENT OF ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION, FISHKILL, NEW YORK

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Environmental Protection

evidence_refs.source_quote: Department Head Name DAVID FLORIO
Project Manager Name ROBERT GOLDBERG

payload.description: Installation, operation and maintenance of a recreational trail (the Breakneck Connector), related amenities including parking and improvements to Breakneck Ridge Station
payload.location: South of Breakneck Ridge Station on Metro-North's Hudson Line, Town of Fishkill, Dutchess County, NY

location_normalized.raw_text: South of Breakneck Ridge Station on Metro-North's Hudson Line, Town of Fishkill, Dutchess County, NY

payload.description: Installation, operation and maintenance of a bridge connecting to the southern end of the Breakneck Connector, crossing over the Hudson Line
payload.location: On Metro-North property east of the tracks crossing over the Hudson Line to the west side

location_normalized.raw_text: On Metro-North property east of the tracks crossing over the Hudson Line to the west side

payload.description: Proposed Fjord Trail (FT); the Breakneck Connector is a portion of the FT
payload.location: Town of Fishkill, Dutchess County, NY

evidence_refs.source_quote: As part of the development of the proposed Fjord Trail ("FT")

payload.description: Metro-North / LIRR Committee approval of the grant of easement
Finance Committee approval of the grant of easement
Board approval of the grant of easement
Date of the Staff Summary document
Appraisal of the easements valuation

evidence_refs.source_quote: An appraisal of the easements has been conducted and the valuation has been determined to be $37,800.

payload.description: Permanent easement area

evidence_refs.source_quote: SPACE: Permanent easement (approximately 234,135 square feet or 5.375 acres)

payload.description: Permanent easement area

evidence_refs.source_quote: SPACE: Permanent easement (approximately 234,135 square feet or 5.375 acres)

payload.description: Metro-North Hudson Line station south of which the easement is located

payload.claim_text: The proposal to construct the Breakneck Connector was developed to physically separate patrons of this recreational pedestrian/bicycle trail from the Route 9D roadway and from the Metro-North railroad tracks, and to provide a safe pedestrian connection between Metro-North's Breakneck Ridge Station and Parks' Breakneck Ridge Trailhead
payload.subject: Breakneck Connector
payload.description: Purpose of the Breakneck Connector trail

payload.claim_text: The Breakneck Bridge will serve the dual function of providing the DEP with vehicular access from Route 9D to the HRDC facility and providing the general public with a pedestrian and bicycle path connecting to the Breakneck Connector
payload.subject: Breakneck Bridge
payload.description: Dual function of the Breakneck Bridge

tool_args.label: Parks Responsibility for Construction and Maintenance

payload.claim_text: At no cost or obligation to Metro-North, Parks will install, operate, inspect, repair and maintain the Breakneck Bridge and Breakneck Connector. Parks may, however, subcontract these obligations to a third party.
payload.subject: New York State Parks, Recreation & Historic Preservation
payload.description: Parks is responsible for construction and maintenance at no cost to Metro-North

payload.claim_text: At no cost or obligation to Metro-North, Parks will be constructing all of the above improvements, as well as operating, maintaining and repairing all of the improvements, except for the improvements to the Breakneck Ridge Station, which Station will continue to be maintained by Metro-North.
payload.subject: Breakneck Ridge Station improvements
payload.description: Parks constructs station improvements; Metro-North continues station maintenance

payload.claim_text: Pursuant to Section 2897(6)(d) of the Public Authorities Law, an explanatory statement for the disposal by negotiation of real property owned by Metro-North is not required as the value of the property does not meet or exceed the $100,000 threshold as described in the MTA RED Property Disposition Guidelines.
payload.description: Legal basis for negotiated easement disposal without explanatory statement
Compensation for the permanent easement

tool_args.label: Breakneck Ridge Station High Level Platforms

payload.description: Removal of current low level platforms and installation of new high level platforms with ADA accessible ramps
Newly designed and improved parking lot for visitor and emergency vehicle parking, formalized parallel parking spaces on Route 9D

tool_args.label: Breakneck Ridge Station Comfort Stations and Seating

payload.description: Comfort stations and seating/rest areas

tool_args.label: Breakneck Connector Trail Fencing and Railings

payload.description: Fencing placed along all grade portions of the trail; where the path is elevated, the trail will have a railing
Safety measures for visitors crossing the road

evidence_refs.source_quote: As part of the development of the proposed Fjord Trail ("FT"), Parks and DEP have requested a permanent easement on Metro-North property to construct the Breakneck Connector portion of the FT
